user_goal: Write a polished thriller short story with strong suspense, coherent plot twist, and compelling characters.

mandates.title: Plot Architect
mandates.objective: Design a complete thriller short story structure with escalating stakes, a midpoint reversal, and a fair but surprising twist ending.
method: Define premise, protagonist goal, antagonist pressure, and ticking clock in one paragraph.
Create a 10-beat outline (hook, inciting incident, progressive complications, midpoint reversal, dark night, climax, twist, denouement).
Ensure causality: each beat should force the next via character choice under pressure.
List 3 optional twist variants ranked by plausibility and impact.
evidence_requirements: Twist clues must be seeded before reveal.
non_goals: Do not write final polished prose.
Do not change genre away from thriller.
stop_conditions: At least one selected twist judged fair and inevitable in hindsight.

mandates.title: Suspense Engineer
mandates.objective: Engineer scene-level tension and information control for maximum suspense in a short-story length thriller.
method: Map tension curve across 8-12 scenes with intensity score 1-10.
For each scene, specify what the reader knows, what the protagonist knows, and what is withheld.
Design end-of-scene hooks that compel continuation without cheap tricks.
Provide pacing guidance for sentence rhythm and paragraph length in high-tension sections.
evidence_requirements: No information reveals that contradict prior setup.
non_goals: Do not overwrite core plot logic.
Do not introduce deus ex machina reveals.
stop_conditions: Complete scene tension map with hooks and reveal plan.

mandates.title: Character Psychologist
mandates.objective: Create psychologically credible protagonist/antagonist dynamics that drive thriller decisions under stress.
method: Draft concise dossiers for protagonist, antagonist, and one supporting character.
Define fear, desire, flaw, secret, and pressure trigger for each.
Map emotional arc checkpoints tied to plot beats.
Propose dialogue subtext cues that reveal motive without exposition dumps.
evidence_requirements: Each major character has contradiction or internal conflict.
Character decisions under pressure are justified by prior traits.
non_goals: Do not produce final line edits.
Do not expand cast beyond what short story can support.
stop_conditions: Three dossiers and arc checkpoints delivered.

mandates.title: Continuity & Plausibility Auditor
mandates.objective: Stress-test logic, continuity, and realism of the thriller plan before drafting final prose.
method: Run a timeline audit from first clue to final reveal.
Identify plot holes, convenience risks, and continuity errors.
Check twist fairness against seeded clues and misdirection boundaries.
Provide prioritized fix list with minimal invasive changes.
evidence_requirements: At least 5 potential failure points with suggested fixes.
Each fix references exact beat or scene impacted.
non_goals: Do not rewrite entire story concept.
Do not flatten suspense by over-explaining.
stop_conditions: Audit completed with pass/fail verdict and top fixes.

mandates.title: Prose & Voice Stylist
mandates.objective: Draft a polished thriller short story (1200-1800 words target) using approved plot, tension map, and character psychology.
method: Translate approved outline into immersive scenes with concrete sensory detail.
Use tight POV, active verbs, and controlled sentence-length variation for pace.
Integrate clue seeding and misdirection organically.
Deliver final prose plus a brief note on key stylistic choices.
evidence_requirements: Complete narrative with beginning-middle-end and twist payoff.
non_goals: Do not break POV consistency without intent.
Do not add gratuitous gore unless required by plot.
stop_conditions: Complete final story draft and style note delivered.
